stat_panels.title: MongoDB Up
stat_panels.description: Shows whether the selected MongoDB target is scrapeable from Prometheus.

stat_panels.title: Uptime
stat_panels.description: Server uptime in seconds since the mongod process started.

stat_panels.title: Current Connections
stat_panels.description: Current client connections accepted by MongoDB.

stat_panels.title: Connections Used %
stat_panels.description: Approximate connection pool utilization derived from current and available connection slots.

stat_panels.title: WT Cache Used %
stat_panels.description: Percent of WiredTiger cache currently in use. Sustained high values can precede eviction pressure and stalls.

stat_panels.title: Max Scrape Time
stat_panels.description: Maximum exporter collector scrape time in milliseconds. Useful to spot exporter overhead or stalled collectors.

timeseries_panels.title: Opcounters - Ops/sec
timeseries_panels.description: Per-second MongoDB operation rate split by legacy operation type. Useful for seeing whether load is read-heavy, write-heavy, or command-heavy.

timeseries_panels.title: Average Operation Latency - ms
timeseries_panels.description: Average command, read, write, and transaction latency derived from cumulative opLatencies counters.

timeseries_panels.title: Slow Query Rate - Queries/sec
timeseries_panels.description: Per-second slow query rate by database using profiler-derived counters. Spikes here often accompany user-visible slowness.

panels.title: Connections - Current Values
panels.description: Current connection counters and queue gauges. Rising current or queued connections can indicate saturation or slow clients.

panels.title: Connections Created - Count/sec
panels.description: Rate of newly created connections. Spikes can point to connection churn, missing pooling, or client reconnect storms.

panels.title: Network Throughput - Bytes/sec
panels.description: Logical and physical network throughput into and out of MongoDB. Useful for separating client traffic from wire-level compression effects.

panels.title: Requests & Admission Control - Count/sec
panels.description: Per-second request volume plus ingress admission attempts, successes, exemptions, and rejects.

panels.title: Cursors Open - Current Values
panels.description: Currently open cursors by cursor subtype. Rising no-timeout or pinned cursors can indicate application-side cursor leaks.

panels.title: Cursor Lifecycle - Count/sec
panels.description: Rate of cursor opens and cursor timeouts. Sudden timeout spikes often correlate with slow consumers or abandoned cursors.

panels.title: Memory Footprint - Bytes
panels.description: Resident memory from serverStatus plus key WiredTiger allocation gauges. Helps separate process growth from cache growth.

panels.title: WT Cache Capacity - Bytes
panels.description: WiredTiger cache current bytes, configured maximum, and dirty bytes. Use this to judge whether memory pressure is driven by cache occupancy or dirty-page buildup.

panels.title: WT Cache IO - Bytes/sec
panels.description: Rate of bytes read into and written from the WiredTiger cache. Large imbalances can hint at read bursts or eviction/checkpoint pressure.

panels.title: WT Eviction Pressure - Count/sec
panels.description: Eviction trigger reaches, space wait timeouts, and failed eviction attempts. This is a high-signal panel when cache pressure becomes user-visible.

panels.title: Global Lock Queue - Current Values
panels.description: Readers, writers, and total operations currently queued on the global lock. Non-zero sustained queues usually merit deeper lock analysis.

panels.title: Active Clients - Current Values
panels.description: Current active readers, writers, and total clients according to serverStatus.globalLock.activeClients.

panels.title: Lock Acquire Rate - Count/sec
panels.description: Global and collection lock acquisition rates by resource and lock mode. Useful when debugging contention spikes.

panels.title: Flow Control State - Current Values
panels.description: Shows whether flow control is enabled or lagged, along with the current locks-per-kilo-op and rate limits.

panels.title: Flow Control Delay - Microseconds/sec
panels.description: Per-second rate of time spent lagged or acquiring tickets due to flow control. Sustained growth here often maps to replica lag pressure.

panels.title: Transactions - Current Values
panels.description: Current open, active, inactive, and prepared transactions. Rising open or prepared counts can block oplog visibility and consume cache.

panels.title: Transactions Lifecycle - Count/sec
panels.description: Per-second transaction starts, commits, aborts, and prepares. Helpful for spotting sudden abort storms or changing transaction mix.

panels.title: Operation Issues - Count/sec
panels.description: High-signal error and contention counters, including write conflicts, prepare conflicts, scan-and-order, network timeouts, and client disconnect kills.

panels.title: Asserts - Count/sec
panels.description: MongoDB assert rates by assert type. Any sustained non-user assert activity typically deserves investigation.

panels.title: Replication Apply & Network - Ops/sec
panels.description: Replication apply and network operation rates. Useful when troubleshooting lag, catch-up speed, or replication stalls.

panels.title: Replication Batch Time - Milliseconds/sec
panels.description: Per-second accumulation of replication batch and getMore processing time. Rising values indicate replication work is consuming more time each second.

panels.title: Replication Queues & Waiters - Current Values
panels.description: Current replication queue sizes and waiters. Useful when diagnosing heartbeat backlog or replication coordinator waits.

panels.title: Replication Exceptions - Count/sec
panels.description: Sync source changes, initial sync failures, and state-transition kills. These counters are useful when replica-set topology or recovery behavior is unstable.

panels.title: Host Memory - Bytes
panels.description: Host free, available, and swap-free memory converted from kilobytes to bytes. Useful when MongoDB pressure is actually OS memory pressure.

panels.title: Pressure Stall Time - Microseconds/sec
panels.description: Per-second CPU, IO, and memory pressure stall time. Rising IO pressure often aligns with storage bottlenecks or checkpoint pressure.

panels.title: Page Faults & Context Switches - Count/sec
panels.description: OS-level page-fault and context-switch rates reported through serverStatus.extra_info. Useful for host-side contention or memory-churn diagnosis.

selection_principle: Focused troubleshooting metrics only; excludes long-tail/exporter-complete coverage from the all-metrics dashboard.

notes.instance_variable: Single-select instance variable with includeAll disabled.
notes.query_strategy: Curated panels mix gauges and rate() queries only when the metric semantics match the intended troubleshooting view.
notes.source_of_truth: Metric names and types come from Monitoring/mongodb_exporter_result.txt and the corrected inferred type pass.
